summary: Both keys encode to 1 byte (int 0 = 0x00, empty tstr = 0x60). Bytewise: 0x00 < 0x60 — integer key sorts first. Under §4.2.1 length-first the lengths tie and tie-break is bytewise, so this case alone does NOT distinguish the profiles — paired with R3-mixed-key-disagreement it exercises the same map for §4.2.2 sort.
